answer: Vietnam's population in 2025 is estimated at around 98 million by the World Bank. The forecast was revised to 6.6% growth for 2025. The World Bank's latest projections are based on economic conditions and trade policies.

results.title: World Bank forecasts Vietnam's GDP growth at 5.8% in 2025
results.snippet: World Bank forecasts Vietnam's GDP growth at 5.8% in 2025. **Vietnam’s gross domestic product (GDP) growth is forecast to moderate to 5.8% in 2025 due to increased trade policy uncertainty, according to the World Bank (WB)'s latest East Asia and Pacific Economic Update.**. Uncertainty may also further weaken consumer confidence and spending which has lagged GDP growth in recent years, the WB said, highlighting financial sector vulnerabilities persist with the average loan-loss coverage ratio among 26 banks at 83% compared to 150% in 2022. The bank stated that given the country’s exposure to the external environment, stronger-than-expected distortions in trade policy could adversely impact exports and growth. The bank predicted that Vietnam's medium-term growth outlook remains positive, with GDP growth estimated to rebound to 6.1% in 2026 and 6.4% in 2027. To realise this potential, Vietnam needs a more stable international economic environment, coupled with domestic reform efforts aimed at increasing productivity, investing in human capital, and greening the economy./.

results.title: World Bank Cuts Vietnam's 2025 Growth Forecast Amid Tariff ...
results.snippet: The World Bank on Monday trimmed its 2025 growth forecast for Vietnam to 6.6% from 6.8% in March and well below the government's 8.3%-8.5%
